Investing in trends. Avoid investing in the latest greatest technologies. Look, you're going to pass up some huge hit. Some home run grand slam investment. I've done it for 30 years. There's three companies I should have gone all in that I didn't. But you know what? There was one thing I did where I went all in that changed everything for me. And I can't have it all. Warren Buffett invested in electricity, railroads, banks, insurance, soft drinks, food, companies, candy. And he swore he would never invest in technology until he bought it into IBM. And IBM is morphing itself away from technology and into a service of corporations and will never go away. Do not get lured in by the latest, greatest, hottest technology. They come today, most are going tomorrow, and very few change the world forever. Don't get on the roller coaster ride. There's uncertainty. You're scared. They go up, they go down, and when they go down, you get scared and you sell. And that money's gone forever and you can't make it back. Take the longer, slower ride that guarantees the arrival of wealth. Wealth, real money, long-term money. Remember, you don't get rich quick and you definitely don't get wealthy ever quick. They don't even pitch that program.
